title: Sonnets VIII
content:
And you as well must die, beloved dust,
             And all your beauty stand you in no stead;
             This flawless, vital hand, this perfect head,
             This body of flame and steel, before the gust
             Of Death, or under his autumnal frost,
             Shall be as any leaf, be no less dead
             Than the first leaf that fell,--this wonder fled.
             Altered, estranged, disintegrated, lost.
             Nor shall my love avail you in your hour.
             In spite of all my love, you will arise
             Upon that day and wander down the air
             Obscurely as the unattended flower,
             It mattering not how beautiful you were,
             Or how beloved above all else that dies.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edna St. Vincent Millay, titled "Sonnets VIII", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
